content.text:
# Code Review — `semantic-provider-contract` branch

Reviewed the 4 commits ahead of `origin/HEAD` plus the uncommitted working-tree changes. Focus: correctness, security, and simplification opportunities.

## Overview

The branch lands a Phase 1 semantic-provider contract:
- New `snapshot/symbols/edges/capabilities/doctor --json/version --json` commands.
- New `internal/sem/provider.go` building NDJSON records (header, files, symbols, relations) with stable compound symbol IDs.
- Uncommitted additions: `--no-network` flag, `no_egress: true` in `doctor --json`, and GitHub remote detection that promotes `repo_key` from `local/<name>` to `gh/<owner>/<repo>`.

Test coverage is reasonable for the happy paths (NDJSON shape, dirty-tree exclusion, no-HEAD warning, unsupported-language partial failures, Go import scanning, GitHub repo key).

## Findings — correctness / security

### 1. (Medium) Silent symbol-ID instability across the repo-key change
`internal/sem/provider.go:178,658-708`

Symbol IDs are `<repo-key>:<language>:<file-path>:<kind>:<qualified-name>` and the spec advertises this as a stable identity governed by `stable_id_version` (`compound-v1`). The uncommitted change makes `repoKey` switch from `local/<basename>` to `gh/<owner>/<repo>` whenever a GitHub remote is found — every ID changes silently, but `StableSymbolIDVersion` stays `"compound-v1"`.

Any downstream brain that has already snapshotted a repo with the `local/` key will see every symbol as new on the next snapshot. Either:
- Bump `StableSymbolIDVersion` to e.g. `compound-v2`, or
- Document the migration semantics in `docs/semantic_provider_requirements.md` (the "stable across content edits but breaks across file moves" wording does not cover this case), or
- Emit a warning record when the upgrade transitions a repo.

### 2. (Medium) `--no-network` is parsed but never enforced
`internal/cli/root.go:213-243`, `internal/cli/root_test.go:141-167`

`providerFlags.NoNetwork` is stored and never read. `runProviderRecords` does not forward it to `BuildProviderSnapshot`, and the snapshot path makes no network calls regardless. The test only proves the flag is accepted.

The provider is local-only today, so behavior is fine — but the flag misleads the reader and any future contributor who adds a non-local feature (e.g., remote grammars) won't notice that `--no-network` should also be gating it. Two reasonable fixes:
- Drop the field/test until there is something to gate, or
- Plumb it through and reject (or skip) any feature whose `FeaturesRequiringNetworkAccess` entry is `true` — this is exactly what `entire-brain` will use the flag for.

### 3. (Low/Medium) `O(F · S_file · S · B)` CALLS-edge scan
`internal/sem/provider.go:402-422`, with `containsIdentifier` at `internal/sem/dependents.go:106`

For each `from` symbol the code loops every entry in `symbolsByShortName` and re-tokenizes `block` via `identifierBoundary.FindAllString(...)` inside `containsIdentifier`. For a 5k-symbol repo this is ~25M regex passes plus tokenization. A one-line simplification: build the token set per block once, then look up `tokens[name]`. The existing `tokenSet` helper in `analyze.go:246` already does this.

### 4. (Low) `githubRepoKey` rejects trailing-slash URLs
`internal/sem/provider.go:687-708`

`https://github.com/owner/repo/` survives `TrimSuffix(".git")` with the trailing slash intact; `(.+)` captures `repo/`, which then fails the `strings.Contains(name, "/")` guard. Trim trailing `/` before applying the regexes. Minor, but the function returns the silent fallback on a URL form `git clone` accepts.

### 5. (Low) `githubRemoteURLs` swallows all errors
`internal/sem/provider.go:679-685`

Any `git config` failure (corrupted config, permission error, exec failure) is indistinguishable from "no remotes" and we fall back to `local/<basename>`. If a remote exists but the lookup blew up, the snapshot quietly produces a different repo key than intended. Consider returning the error to `BuildProviderSnapshot` so it can append a warning like `E_REMOTE_LOOKUP`.

### 6. (Low) Redundant `no_egress: true` and `phase_1_local_only` keys
`internal/cli/root.go:105-128`

`doctor --json` now advertises both `"no_egress": true` and a `"phase_1_local_only"` object with six `false` flags. Consumers (and the brain integration tests) have to know to check either. Pick one canonical shape — the `phase_1_local_only` block already encodes per-capability granularity.

### 7. (Low) `UnsupportedButDetectedLanguages` is permanently `[]`
`internal/sem/provider.go:114-152`

`CapabilityReport.UnsupportedButDetectedLanguages` is initialized to an empty slice and never populated. The runtime equivalent (per-file `E_UNSUPPORTED_LANGUAGE` partial failures) already exists. Either populate it from the static `unsupportedLanguageHint` list (Java/Kotlin/Ruby/etc.) so consumers can discover detectability up front, or drop the field. The current state is misleading capability advertising.

### 8. (Low) Heuristic noise in HANDLES_ROUTE / HANDLES_TOOL
`internal/sem/provider.go:591-606`

- `routeLiterals` matches any quoted token starting with `/`, including comments like `// /foo` and arbitrary path strings in fixtures. Will emit `HANDLES_ROUTE` edges for unrelated string constants.
- `looksLikeToolHandler` keys on tokens `tool` + `(handler|execute|schema)`. Easy false positives on words like `toolbar`, `pool`, `schemamatic`, etc. — `tokenSet` requires identifier-token boundary, so `toolbar` would not match `tool`. Worth a short comment about the heuristic so downstream readers don't treat the confidence values (`0.58`, `0.7`) as ground truth.

These are flagged in the records via `Confidence < 1` and the `Reason` field, so it's tolerable, but consider documenting these as best-effort hints in the requirements doc.

### 9. (Nit) `runDoctor` interleaves JSON-state and text emission
`internal/cli/root.go:100-168`

The function builds `report` and emits text in two interleaved branches across five `if !asJSON` blocks. Refactor to build the full map first, then format once at the end — the function is becoming hard to extend and the `repo_root`/`repo_error` early-return path duplicates the encoder call.

## Findings — simplify

- `githubRemoteURLs` (`provider.go:679-685`) is a 4-line wrapper that only swallows the error. Either inline at the single caller or have it return the error so callers can warn.
- The four regexes in `githubRepoKey` (`provider.go:687-708`) can collapse to one: a host/path normalize step (strip `git@github.com:` / `^(https?|ssh)://(git@)?github\.com/`) then split on `/`. Equivalent behavior, less duplication.
- `scanGoImports` (`provider.go:541-568`) duplicates the quoted-string capture group between `singleImport` and `blockImport`. A single regex on the trimmed line works for both states once you've decided you're inside a block.
- `WriteSymbolsNDJSON` and `WriteRelationsNDJSON` (`provider.go:305-321`) share identical structure with `WriteSnapshotNDJSON`. Optional, but a small `writeRecords(out, records ...any)` helper would remove the three-way duplication.

## Tests

- New tests cover JSON/NDJSON shapes, GitHub SSH/HTTPS detection, fallback, dirty-tree exclusion, missing HEAD, unsupported language, and Go import scanner.
- Gap: nothing pins `stable_id_version` against a fixed golden ID. A regression test that asserts a known symbol ID byte-for-byte would catch the silent migration described in finding #1.
- Gap: no test asserts `git rev-parse` failure cases other than "no git" — a `partial_failures` aggregation test (multiple bad files → `degraded` vs `unsafe`) would lock in the threshold semantics.

## Security

No new attack surface introduced:
- All git access uses `exec.CommandContext` with separate args (no shell).
- `workingTreeFiles` skips `.git`, `node_modules`, etc., and skips symlinked entries.
- No network code paths added; `doctor` claims match the implementation.
- The Claude/Codex hook configs guard with `command -v entire` and pass no untrusted strings to `sh -c`.

One observation: hooks invoke `entire hooks ...` with whatever `entire` is first on `PATH`. That's standard for these integrations, but worth noting if this repo is ever opened in an untrusted workspace.

## Recommendation

Address #1 (symbol-ID stability) and #2 (`--no-network` enforcement or removal) before merging — those are contract issues that other teams will rely on. The rest are tractable follow-ups.
